title: salix × mollissima
intro: Salix × mollissima, also known as white willow or weeping willow, is a deciduous tree or shrub that belongs to the Salicaceae family, being a hybrid of the white willow (Salix alba) and the black willow (Salix nigra). The bark and leaves of Salix × mollissima have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The medicinal properties of Salix × mollissima are due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help alleviate headaches, reduce fever, and relieve pain. Herbal preparations of Salix × mollissima include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, gout, and menstrual cramps.
